name: Southwestern Biscuits- p Cooking Class
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 450°F.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, baking powder, salt and black or cayenne pepper.
3. Cut in the vegetable shortening using a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture has the consistency of coarse crumbs.
4. Add the diced canned green chilies, grated cheddar cheese, and milk to the dough. If necessary, add more milk so the dough is pliable but still wet.
5. Turn the dough onto a heavily floured surface and knead it lightly (8 to 10 times). Do not overwork the dough.
6. Roll the dough to an even thickness of 3/4-to-1 inch. Use a 2-inch round cutter (the diameter of a juice can) to cut out biscuits, dipping the cutter in flour between each cut to prevent sticking.
7. Place the biscuits on an ungreased cookie sheet and bake in the center of the oven for 12 to 15 minutes, or until golden brown. Serve immediately, or bake up to 2 hours in advance and reheat for 5 minutes at 300°F. Makes 12 to 14.
